issues.title: [Help wanted] Default window decorations color
issues.next_action_reason: Re-anchor this popular help-wanted thread: explain the RAIL server-side titlebar vs GTK_THEME distinction and invite a scoped PR; add the 'help wanted' label to match the title.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for keeping this alive — it stays one of the most-requested decoration issues. To summarize where it stands for newcomers: WSLg draws server-side (RDP RAIL) decorations for the title bar, so an app's own GTK_THEME does not control the titlebar color; apps that draw client-side decorations (many GTK apps) follow the theme, while apps that rely on the WM/server titlebar (JetBrains, xcalc) get the default light frame. This is tagged help-wanted — a PR that plumbs a dark-titlebar preference (or honors the host's dark-mode / a `.wslgconfig` setting) through the RAIL decoration path would be very welcome. If anyone investigating wants pointers on where the titlebar color is chosen in the compositor, reply here and we can help scope it.

issues.title: Clipboard integration on libweston may cause race condition and crash the weston process
issues.next_action_reason: Confirmed crash with community root-cause; acknowledge, route to weston-mirror, invite PR.
issues.suggested_reply: Thank you @tumugin and @abjfh30 — this is exceptional triage. You've traced the crash to `clipboard_data_source_send()` replacing `source->data_source_fd` while the old `transfer_event_source` is still registered against the stale fd, so the old callback fires on a closed/replaced fd. That's a concrete, fixable race in the RDP clipboard backend (`weston-mirror` `rdpclip.c`). We'll take this to the weston-mirror repo — @abjfh30, if you're up for it, a PR guarding/unregistering the stale `transfer_event_source` before reassigning the fd would be very welcome. Marking this as a confirmed crash.
